description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:19.03.1-git image with a docker:19.03.1-dind service, define APPLICATION_REPOSITORY, APPLICATION_TAG, DOCKER_DRIVER, and DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR variables, and three stages: build, test, and release. In the build stage add a docker_image job that runs `docker build -t "$APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$APPLICATION_TAG" .`, conditionally logs in to $CI_REGISTRY with $CI_REGISTRY_USER and $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD, and pushes the image with `docker push "$APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$APPLICATION_TAG"`. In the test stage add a codequality job that pulls the built image, tags it as codeclimate/codeclimate-gocyclo, runs `docker run --rm --env CODECLIMATE_CODE="$(pwd)" --volume "$(pwd)":/code --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock --volume /tmp/cc:/tmp/cc codeclimate/codeclimate:0.85.5 analyze -f json --dev | tee gl-code-quality-report.json`, then if the JSON file is not empty installs jq via `apk add -U --no-cache jq` and pretty‑prints it, and stores gl-code-quality-report.json as a codequality artifact for one week. In the release stage add a docker tagged image job that runs only on Git tags, pulls the application image, tags it to `$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_TAG`, conditionally logs in to the registry, and pushes the tagged image. Also add a promote to latest job that runs manually on tags or the master branch, pulls the application image, tags it to `$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest`, conditionally logs in, and pushes the latest tag.
